summary.headline: ADM reports fourth quarter and full-year 2023 earnings with adjusted EPS of $6.98.
summary.narrative_summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland Company (ADM) reported fourth quarter and full-year 2023 financial results, with adjusted earnings per share of $6.98 for the year. The company's full-year segment operating profit was $6,244 million, down 6% from the prior year. Key factors included lower margins in Ag Services and Oilseeds, reduced equity earnings from Wilmar, and impacts from unplanned downtime at the Decatur complex. The company also announced an additional $2 billion in share repurchases.

drivers: record export volumes out of Brazil
concerns: lower margins due to global grain and oilseed supply recovery

drivers: higher pricing and lower input costs in Starches Sweeteners
strong export demand and steady domestic blending in Vantage Corn Processing
concerns: cycling one-time benefits from USDA Biofuel Producer Recovery Program

drivers: lower volumes and increased manufacturing costs in Human Nutrition
lower amino acid margins and sub-segment volumes in Animal Nutrition
concerns: deconsolidation and write-down of a joint venture

drivers: higher Captive Insurance results on new programs

facilities_mentioned.name: Decatur complex
facilities_mentioned.context: unplanned downtime negatively impacted fourth quarter EPS

facilities_mentioned.name: Decatur East
facilities_mentioned.context: unplanned downtime negatively impacted fourth quarter EPS and full-year Nutrition segment results

ma_or_capital_allocation.description: Announces additional $2 billion in share repurchases.
